summary: Merged decomp-matching work for melee/vi0501 and sysdolphin/baselib/psdisp. The PR implemented most of un_8031D9F8, fully matched particleSort, and substantially improved psDispParticles by restoring particle batching, cache/register behavior, and more exact GX rendering branches. decomp-dev reported matched code +1048 bytes, particleSort 92.88% -> 100.00%, un_8031D9F8 0.00% -> 98.83%, and psDispParticles 39.03% -> 58.06%. It also deleted a stale target_funcs.txt file containing mncharsel targets.

key_files.role: Implemented un_8031D9F8 and added required camera, player, stage, ground, item, collision, GObj/JObj includes and state.
Matched particleSort and improved psDispParticles with batching helpers, cache hoisting, channel-state branch reshaping, color math casts, and more exact GX FIFO rendering.
Deleted a 13-line target list for mnCharSel functions.

what_changed: Added static HSD_GObj* un_804A2E98[4] in vi0501.c and implemented un_8031D9F8(CharacterKind char_kind, int costume, int spawn_mode, int spawn_count).

un_8031D9F8 now initializes camera/collision/ground/stage/items/demo player state, creates the requested main character plus three Kirby demo players, samples their JObj translations at animation frame 140.0f, scales positions by 1.4285715f * un_804D6F80 into grLib_801C9A10() slots, resets animation to 0.0f, and starts AX audio calls.

particleSort was rewritten at the tail-stitching stage from long manual bucket increments into two explicit loops over buckets 0..7 and 8..15, then links the two sorted lists and updates hsd_804D0908[arg0], cache->xAC[arg0], arg2, and arg3.
Added getColorMatAmb_inner and getColorMatAmb_noinline wrapper shims and changed psDispParticles call sites from getColorMatAmb to getColorMatAmb_noinline.

Added static inline psDispSubPoint and psDispSubPointTrail helpers. They batch up to 16 compatible consecutive particles into GX_POINTS or GX_LINES, manage cached point/line width globals, and write positions/colors/texture coordinates directly through GXWGFifo.

psDispParticles now hoists cache = (f32*) &HSD_PSDisp_804D0FC0 before the main loop, removes repeated per-branch cache reassignments, reshapes GX channel-control logic away from a switch, and uses signed intermediate casts for several color multiplications.

psDispParticles gained larger changes for DispPoint, Trail, DirVec, Tornado, appsrt, form, and non-form rendering paths, including projection/view-matrix direction-vector math, PSMTXRotAxisRad axis rotation, form-driven trail primitives, and corrected quad vertex layouts.
Deleted target_funcs.txt, which only listed mnCharSel targets and was not part of the vi0501/psdisp implementation itself.
smart_moves: The squash commit notes that psDispSubPoint and psDispSubPointTrail were declared in psstructs.h but not implemented; restoring those helpers was a high-leverage move because psDispParticles jumped from 39.03% to 58.06%.

Batching compatible particles in local 16-entry buffers matched the original renderer strategy better than drawing each point/line one at a time, while also making the GX_POINTS/GX_LINES FIFO shape explicit.
Hoisting &HSD_PSDisp_804D0FC0 into a cache variable before the hot loop matches the commit message's stated target r26 saved-register behavior and avoids repeated noisy casts/reloads.
The getColorMatAmb wrapper chain is a useful codegen tactic: even tiny static inline pass-through helpers can alter call/inlining shape enough to improve a stubborn rendering function.
Using two clean bucket loops in particleSort made the fixed 16-bucket sort clearer and still produced a full function match, replacing a brittle-looking hand-unrolled sequence.
The vi0501 implementation pulled in real subsystem headers rather than relying on ad hoc declarations, which is safer for matching calls across camera, stage, player, item, ground, and baselib APIs.
decomp_lessons: For renderer decompilation, matching often depends on recovering hidden helper structure and batching behavior, not just rearranging statements inside one giant function.
A fixed-size data structure does not always require hand-unrolled source: particleSort matched after expressing bucket concatenation as loops over 0..7 and 8..15.
Global render/cache structs may need to be held in a saved register across loops. Hoist the pointer once if the target keeps a global cache base live for many branches.

GX FIFO code is sensitive to exact vertex descriptor and format choices: this PR distinguishes GX_VTXFMT0/1 for point/quad paths, GX_VTXFMT2/3 for line/trail color paths, and GX_VTXFMT5 for textured form data.

Trail/DirVec/Tornado direction matching needs target-like projection/view math and sign conventions; simple screen-space current-minus-previous approximations were replaced with cache/projection-based calculations.
Small cast differences matter in color math. The diff changed several u32-style products to signed s32 intermediate shifts before casting back to u8.

For VI/demo setup functions, preserve unresolved types when evidence is weak: spawn_count is still an int cast to u8* counts, and un_804D6F84 keeps a TODO for a possible ViCharaDesc rather than inventing a struct.
Stack padding and unused pad locals can be legitimate matching tools; un_8031D9F8 uses PAD_STACK(4) plus dummy s32 locals to shape the stack frame.
naming_conventions: Unresolved VI symbols keep the project style un_######## / un_######## arrays, e.g. un_8031D9F8, un_804A2E98, un_804D6F80, and un_804D6F84.
Potential-but-unconfirmed data names are marked with TODO comments rather than renamed prematurely, as shown by un_804D6F84's @todo #ViCharaDesc comment.
Baselib particle helpers use lowerCamelCase names such as particleSort, getColorMatAmb_inner, getColorMatAmb_noinline, psDispSubPoint, and psDispSubPointTrail.
Subsystem calls retain existing module prefixes and capitalization: Camera_80028B9C, Ground_801C0378, Stage_802251E8, Item_80266FA8, Player_SetPlayerCharacter, and Player_SetCostumeId.
Game constants and enum/flag names are reused directly: PURA, CKIND_KIRBY, Gm_PKind_Demo, DispPoint, DispTexture, DispLighting, Trail, PrimEnv, Tornado, DirVec, TexFlipS, and TexFlipT.
HSD object access follows existing GObj/JObj conventions: HSD_GObj*, HSD_JObj*, GET_JOBJ, HSD_JObjReqAnimAll, HSD_JObjAnimAll, and HSD_JObjGetTranslation2.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: decomp-dev evidence: particleSort became a new 100.00% match, vi0501 .sdata became 100.00%, un_8031D9F8 improved to 98.83%, and psDispParticles improved to 58.06%.
Artificial temporaries such as HSD_Particle** new_var and temp_r29 = (new_var = &hsd_804D0908[arg0]) were used in particleSort to influence register allocation/expression shape.

The getColorMatAmb_inner/getColorMatAmb_noinline pass-through wrappers are codegen shims even though they are declared static inline; they changed psDispParticles call structure without changing semantics.
psDispSubPoint and psDispSubPointTrail return the last consumed particle, allowing psDispParticles to assign pp = helper(pp) and skip over a batched run in the linked list.
Point/trail batching flushes exactly at count == 16, with stack arrays Vec3 buf[16] and vbuf/cbuf[16], matching the target's small fixed batch size.
The channel-state GXSetChanCtrl switch was reshaped into nested comparisons around chan_state and DispLighting | Trail, including scoped GXAttnFn calculation only where needed.
Projection-sensitive branches compare against cache[0x18], use reciprocal w values, atan2f, fabsf thresholds, and __frsqrte-based normalization to better match target floating-point code.
The vi0501 function uses PAD_STACK(4), dummy pad locals, float literals like 0.7f, 140.0f, and 1.4285715f, and a cast from int spawn_count to u8* counts to preserve observed codegen.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code 69.20% (+0.03%, +1048 bytes) and matched data 41.20% (+16 bytes). New matches: particleSort 92.88% -> 100.00% and vi0501 .sdata 0.00% -> 100.00%. Improvements: psDispParticles 39.03% -> 58.06%, un_8031D9F8 0.00% -> 98.83%, vi0501 .sdata2 66.67% -> 72.73%, psdisp extabindex 91.67% -> 95.83%.
Reported one remaining regression: main/sysdolphin/baselib/psdisp .sdata2 moved 82.00% -> 77.78% (-4 bytes).
No human PR body, review comments, or review records were present in the slice; reviewer intent should not be inferred.

follow_up_queries: Investigate the psdisp .sdata2 regression reported by decomp-dev: 82.00% -> 77.78%, -4 bytes.
Finish the remaining mismatch in un_8031D9F8; likely areas to inspect are spawn_count typing, ViCharaDesc/un_804D6F84 semantics, and stack/register layout.
Continue psDispParticles from 58.06% by comparing appsrt, form, Trail, DirVec, Tornado, projection-cache, and billboard rotation branches against target assembly.
Verify psDispSubPoint and psDispSubPointTrail declarations in psstructs.h or related headers if future PRs touch particle display prototypes.
Find real names or struct types for un_804A2E98 and the vi0501 character descriptor data once more scene/demo evidence appears.
Keep personal target tracking files out of commits or migrate target lists to the project's accepted tracking mechanism.
